dialogueChunks.intro: The guild banned the sale of moonwort remedies last season. Said they were 'unregulated.' Four children are sick with swamp fever and moonwort is the only thing that works.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: A herbalist outside town has been growing {quest_focus} in secret at {quest_origin}. She knows the guild will have her arrested if they find out. She doesn't care. She said: 'Let them fine me. I'm not watching children die over a regulation.' But she can't deliver the medicine herself: the guild has watchers on the roads.
dialogueChunks.outro: Someone needs to {quest_action} to the families before {quest_time_pressure}. The herbalist has committed a crime: growing and distributing banned substances. She did it because {quest_stakes} matter more to her than her freedom.

playerResponses.text: Sick children outrank guild regulations. I'll make the delivery.
If I'm caught, I go down too. What's the plan?
I'm not running contraband, even for a good reason.

dialogueChunks.intro: A soldier deserted his post three days ago. Not for cowardice. His daughter is dying and the commander wouldn't grant leave.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: He walked away from a garrison of fifty knowing the penalty is death. His daughter has the wasting sickness and he has a remedy that only works if administered by someone who knows the dosage. He's hiding at {quest_origin} with his family. The military patrol is already searching.
dialogueChunks.outro: If someone could {quest_action} and buy him time to treat his daughter, he'll turn himself in willingly. He said: 'They can hang me after she's well. I just need three days.' {quest_threat} won't show mercy, but {quest_stakes} hang on those three days.

playerResponses.text: A father choosing his dying child over a post. I understand. I'll help.
Desertion is desertion. But the child didn't choose this.
I won't obstruct a military investigation.

dialogueChunks.intro: The border embargo means no trade with the settlements across the river. It also means the village on the far bank is starving. One of our merchants has been smuggling food across in secret.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: The merchant's own family lives on this side. If she's caught smuggling {quest_focus}, she loses everything: her license, her home, probably her freedom. She does it because her childhood friend lives across the river with six children and no food. Love doesn't care about border policy.
dialogueChunks.outro: The next shipment needs to cross tonight. She can't make the run herself because {quest_threat} have doubled patrols. She needs someone to {quest_action}. It's illegal, it's dangerous, and it will feed twenty people for a week.

playerResponses.text: Hungry children don't respect borders either. I'll do it.
How many more runs until this becomes unsustainable?
